rule_statement: The grader-visible answer for free-form items is the LAST \boxed{} in the response
why_suspected: Source-code analysis and submission probes both say only the final free-form box survives
submission_confirmation: Probe verified in submission registry and 25_08 Slot 4 behavior
inference_confirmation: Multi-box raw outputs are common across inference runs
web_or_staff_confirmation: Older Hendrycks source matches this claim
judger_confirmation: Old local judger differed from Kaggle here
notes: Safe canonical rule regardless of later lenience update because it is structural

rule_statement: MCQ grading uses the FIRST \boxed{LETTER} rather than the last box
why_suspected: Needed to explain why append-at-end MCQ overrides were no-ops
submission_confirmation: 25_08 Slot 3 no-op and 29_05 full-replace fix confirmed it
inference_confirmation: MCQ bare-letter-only failures appear in run scan
web_or_staff_confirmation: Starter notebook and old grader path documented this distinction
judger_confirmation: Current judger has separate MCQ logic too
notes: This is the single most important MCQ formatting rule

rule_name: Multi-answer must be one box in order
rule_statement: Multi-answer outputs must be represented in ONE box with comma-separated values in the correct slot order
why_suspected: Per-slot boxes and reversed order caused large empirical losses
submission_confirmation: Probe and submission evidence: per-slot boxes catastrophic and order reversal catastrophic
inference_confirmation: Run scan shows multi-box outputs are pervasive
web_or_staff_confirmation: Competition and starter prompt both imply one boxed list
judger_confirmation: Local judger accepts more forms than Kaggle did historically
notes: Structural rule remains high confidence even if numeric equivalence became more adaptive

rule_statement: Collect all boxed subanswers and consolidate them into the final boxed answer when the model undercounts
why_suspected: 79 percent of the audited failures were attributed to undercount and Slot 4 moved the score materially
inference_confirmation: Repo-wide scan found 8417 samples already rich enough to consolidate
judger_confirmation: Current judger is not the authority but also benefits from structurally complete answers
notes: This is the highest-EV confirmed formatting lever in the repo

rule_name: dfrac and tfrac normalize to frac
rule_statement: \dfrac and \tfrac are equivalent to \frac for grader-visible comparison
why_suspected: This is explicit in both Hendrycks and current local judger normalization
submission_confirmation: No leaderboard movement expected because this is redundant
inference_confirmation: Seen throughout inference outputs and sample submission
web_or_staff_confirmation: Supported by Hendrycks source and current local judger code
notes: Keep in conservative because it is safe and audit-friendly

rule_statement: \left and \right plus ordinary spacing are not meaningful for grading
why_suspected: Source code shows these are removed before comparison
submission_confirmation: No dedicated submission needed because source confirms it
inference_confirmation: Appears frequently in raw model outputs
notes: Whitespace-insensitive comma spacing should not consume submission slots

rule_statement: Prefixes like x=5 or D=8 are stripped only when the left-hand side is at most two characters
why_suspected: Explicitly documented in source analysis and current judger cleanup
submission_confirmation: No separate submission probe needed because source is explicit
judger_confirmation: Current judger also strips or normalizes similar patterns
notes: Do not generalize this into multi-char label stripping without evidence

rule_statement: Under the old grader model decimals and fractions were not generally equivalent except for exact 0.5
why_suspected: Historically documented by Hendrycks source and reinforced by Slot 1 fraction wins
source_docs: but the user-provided May 2026 judge update may have changed this
wolfram_confirmation: 25_08 Slot 1 and 29_05 Build 1 previously supported fraction preference
inference_confirmation: Wolfram helped identify mathematically correct fraction targets
web_or_staff_confirmation: Teacher and answer-sheet metadata often surfaced fraction targets
judger_confirmation: Anthony Tong 2026-05-09 said no server-side sympy; user reports May 2026 adaptive update may supersede this
implemented_in: Current judger is clearly more adaptive and sympy-backed than old Hendrycks
last_reviewed: Keep metadata-backed promotion in default and broad conversion only in aggressive until revalidated on current Kaggle judge

rule_statement: Under the old grader model trailing zeros were significant but standalone stripping tested neutral on the public slice
why_suspected: Docs flag many candidate items but empirical submission evidence was neutral overall and the new judge update may have made this less important
wolfram_confirmation: Wolfram occasionally exposed cleaner numeric surfaces
inference_confirmation: Run scan found 4003 trailing-zero outputs across stored samples
web_or_staff_confirmation: No direct web confirmation beyond old Hendrycks analysis
judger_confirmation: Current judger normalizes much more aggressively around numeric expressions
notes: Leave out of conservative/default and keep only in aggressive unless current Kaggle behavior is rechecked

rule_statement: Prefixes like Mean=228 or A=72 are not reliably stripped by the old grader and can cause losses
why_suspected: This explains concrete failures in the search overlay run
submission_confirmation: 25_08 Slot 2 failures on items 20 108 and 139 were attributed to labels
wolfram_confirmation: Wolfram/search often provided raw labeled answers that needed cleaning
inference_confirmation: Inference traces sometimes emit labeled boxed answers
judger_confirmation: Current judger appears more tolerant because it strips text wrappers and parses expressions
notes: Implemented only in aggressive because the current Kaggle update may partially mitigate this but we have not revalidated

rule_statement: Using * instead of canonical math notation could fail under the old grader
why_suspected: Documented as a concrete source of losses in Slot 2 search overlay
submission_confirmation: 25_08 Slot 2 items 104 and 127 cited as losses
inference_confirmation: Appears in some generated symbolic expressions
judger_confirmation: Current judger may parse these expressions symbolically which increases mismatch risk with old docs
notes: Do not auto-rewrite broadly until current judge behavior is revalidated

rule_statement: Large-number commas may or may not be normalized and were historically considered a potential mismatch
why_suspected: Rule appears in older research and strict specs but had little direct empirical evidence
inference_confirmation: Run scan found 302 comma-number outputs across stored samples
judger_confirmation: Current judger likely parses many comma-number forms successfully
notes: Track in registry but do not prioritize without current evidence

rule_statement: Old Hendrycks-style grading did not necessarily strip \mathrm{} and \mathbf{} wrappers while the current local judger does
why_suspected: This is a direct example of repo assumptions diverging across time
inference_confirmation: Wrappers occur in raw outputs and answer sheets
judger_confirmation: Current judger strips these wrappers in normalize_answer
notes: Treat old claims here as stale-risk until current Kaggle behavior is directly rechecked

rule_statement: Some items never produce boxed answers and need a deterministic rescue or override
why_suspected: Repo findings list a small set of high-confidence rescue cases
submission_confirmation: No explicit leaderboard isolation but included in findings
wolfram_confirmation: Some rescue answers came from external math verification
inference_confirmation: Run analysis found 1675 no-box samples and a small high-confidence rescue set
judger_confirmation: Current judger strict-extract discussion shows why boxed extraction matters
notes: Use only when consensus is strong and visible in registry/override evidence

rule_statement: Problem-source clues may imply preferred answer surface such as integer fraction or decimal
why_suspected: This comes from repo research rather than direct submission proof
inference_confirmation: Inferred from problem text patterns and run behavior
judger_confirmation: Current judger does not itself perform source classification
notes: Keep heuristic routing limited to aggressive mode until per-item validation exists

rule_name: Current judge may be more adaptive than old docs
rule_statement: The competition judge was reportedly updated around 2026-05-27 to fix fraction-decimal and related false negatives
why_suspected: This directly conflicts with older repo assumptions that the Kaggle judge is pure Hendrycks string match
source_docs: user-provided competition announcement; judger.py; grading/GRADER_SPEC.md; grading/JUDGER_AND_PUBLIC_SET.md
submission_confirmation: Not yet revalidated with fresh differential submissions
inference_confirmation: No new inference artifact proves or disproves it
web_or_staff_confirmation: User-provided announcement from Kaggle plus current starter-code judger update
judger_confirmation: Current judger.py is plainly more adaptive and sympy-backed than the old Hendrycks model
implemented_in: not yet encoded as a safe default rule
notes: This is the highest-priority audit item before burning remaining submissions on format-only probes
